transcript.content: ugh i just had the absolute worst day at work today. my manager was breathing down my neck the whole time and i feel so exhausted.

It sounds like you experienced a tremendous amount of stress and pressure today. Having a manager constantly on your back can be really draining. Can you talk a bit about what happened? Was it a specific project or just the constant oversight?

it's not even just today, honestly. it feels like it's been weeks of this. every little task is a major crisis and i've just had things piling up on me constantly. i can barely get out of bed in the morning lately because i'm already dreading the sheer amount of crap i have to deal with.

That sounds like a really intense level of persistent work stress. Feeling like every task is a crisis and constantly dealing with piles of work that never seem to end sounds incredibly exhausting. That level of burnout is serious. It's understandable that you're feeling so drained and anxious about just getting out of bed knowing what awaits.
yeah it's just exhausting. honestly idk why i even bother anymore. like what is the point of putting in all this effort just to wake up and do the exact same stressful thing the next day.

It's totally understandable that after so much stress, you'd question the purpose or worth of the effort. The cycle of 'effort -> stress -> exhaustion' feels really defeating. It sounds like you've reached a breaking point where the cost feels too high for the return.
exactly. and it's not even just work, it's everything. nothing really feels worth it lately. i don't have the energy for my friends, my hobbies, nothing. i'm just completely empty.

The burnout is so pervasive that it's spilling over into all areas of your life. The energy drain from work isn't just affecting your job performance, it's impacting your personal life, relationships, and enjoyment. That feeling of being completely empty sounds incredibly isolating and hopeless.

it is really isolating. i just lie in bed at night thinking about how much of a burden i am to everyone around me. honestly sometimes i think everyone would just be better off without me here to drag them down.

That sense of burden and feeling like a burden is incredibly heavy. The isolation and loneliness from burnout can really amplify feelings of worthlessness and even thoughts of self-harm. It's important to recognize that you're struggling with thoughts like 'everyone would be better off without me', which are distressing and should be addressed with professional help.
sorry, i didn't mean to make it sound that serious or scare you. i'm just really tired and overwhelmed tonight, i don't actually mean it like that. i think i just need some sleep.

Thank you for clarifying. I understand how the fatigue and feeling overwhelmed can make even basic conversations feel draining. I appreciate you sharing your experience, and remember, reaching out is the first step, and getting help is often the most important action to begin rebuilding energy and perspective.
Yeah, sleep definitely helps sometimes. I think I'm just going to try to shut my brain off for the night and log off. Thanks for listening to me vent, it did help to just get it out of my head.

It's good you know the importance of sleep and taking time to let your mind quiet down. Venting can be really helpful. I'm glad you felt some relief. Thanks for being honest about the toll this is taking, and remember, talking to a professional can be a powerful way to begin finding ways to reduce the pressure and restore some balance.

Thanks, yeah. I might look into that eventually when I have more energy, but for tonight I think I really just need to close my eyes. I'll probably just listen to some quiet music or something to wind down. Thanks again for being a safe space to talk, it means a lot.

Glad to be a safe space! Quiet music sounds like a great wind-down. If you have a list of things you want to do tomorrow, sometimes starting small is the hardest part. Taking one task off the list, even a tiny one, can be a big win. Good luck getting some much-needed rest!

thanks, yeah, starting small is probably the way to go. i'm honestly too tired to even think about tomorrow's list right now though. i'm just gonna put my phone on silent and try to drift off. goodnight.

Sounds like a good plan. Even a tiny step like just putting your phone away can help signal your brain that it's time to sleep. Good luck getting some rest, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need more help. Night!
thanks. i really appreciate it. goodnight for real this time.
You're very welcome! Thank you for being honest and vulnerable, which is the first step in feeling heard and understood. Night!
haha yeah, thanks. i'm seriously going to sleep now. take care.
Night, take care, and good luck with getting some rest!
thanks again. off to sleep now, bye.
Night! Take care and good luck.
bye!
Night!
haha okay seriously bye for real now! thanks again.
Goodnight! Sleep well!
haha okay, i am actually locking my phone now. for real this time! thanks for being so patient with me. goodnight!
You're very welcome! I'm glad you found it helpful and that you're taking action to sleep. Goodnight!
